original_hypothesis.title: Factory registry can be poisoned with arbitrary pools (addPool without validation), enabling UI/indexer deception and unsafe routing
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Factory.addPool explicitly does not validate that `pool` is a real MagicLP clone of the current implementation nor that it matches base/quote. If the Factory owner key is compromised or operationally misused, arbitrary addresses can be inserted into pools[base][quote], poisoning registries used by UIs/routers/indexers and potentially causing users to route funds to malicious pools.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operational integrity risk: registry is typically a trust anchor for pool discovery. Lack of validation increases blast radius of any operational mistake or compromise.
deployment_assumptions: Off-chain components (UI, indexers, SDKs, path builders) treat Factory.pools as authoritative
Factory owner actions are not protected by additional operational controls beyond Owned
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Factory.addPool comment: 'doesn't check if the pool is valid or if it's already registered.'

proof_of_signal.pattern: addPool onlyOwner calls _addPool without validating `pool` code/parameters; comment confirms no validation

preconditions: Factory owner (or governance controlling owner) performs malicious/erroneous addPool
Integrations rely on the registry without verifying pool bytecode/immutables

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Registry integrity depends entirely on owner correctness; no on-chain invariant enforcement
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: any time after malicious pool insertion

operational_questions: Do official routing components only use Factory registry, or do they validate pool bytecode/implementation pointer?
Is there monitoring on addPool/removePool events and automated verification?
What is the ownership setup for Factory on Blast (multisig, timelock, etc.)?
validation_requirements: Attempt to route via registry to a non-MagicLP pool in a test harness
Review production indexer logic for trust in LogPoolAdded events
Assess if Router ever uses Factory registry (it currently does not)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Insert malicious pool into Factory registry for a popular base/quote
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Ensure integrators discover and route users to it
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Steal funds via malicious pool behavior or fee extraction

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: registry integrity lost; user routing compromised
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: fund loss, trading disruptions, reputational damage
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: medium (remove pool, but user losses irreversible)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: unexpected LogPoolAdded entries; pools with mismatched tokens
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: swaps routed to pools with abnormal pricing/fees
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: owner adding pools outside normal deployment pipeline

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
